ai_scope_discriminator: A snapshot is only in AI scope if the cluster/snapshot actually holds vector or ML data, not generic logs/docs. Confirm via NAMES ONLY, no restore: (1) GET /_snapshot/{repo}/_all returns each snapshot's `indices[]` array; flag index names that read AI/ML (e.g. *embeddings*, *vectors*, *-knn, *rag*, *semantic*, *.ml-* , .ml-anomalies, .ml-config, .inference, *elser*). (2) For a live cluster, GET /{index}/_mapping (or GET /_mapping) and look for field types `dense_vector`, `sparse_vector`, or OpenSearch `knn_vector` (plus `index.knn: true` in /{index}/_settings) and ELSER/inference fields -> positive AI confirmation. (3) GET /_ingest/pipeline or GET /_ml/trained_models (ES) / GET /_plugins/_ml/models (OpenSearch) reveals deployed embedding/inference models. Absence of all three = generic substrate (plain text/log backup), OUT of AI scope per the substrate-vs-AI gate. Snapshot manifest alone gives index names; mapping read upgrades it from "named like AI" to "is AI vector store."

dorks.strict: "You Know, for Search" port:9200 "cluster_name" -"security_exception" -"missing authentication credentials"

fingerprint_markers: GET /_snapshot returns JSON keyed by repo name with "type":"fs"|"s3"|"gcs"|"azure"|"url" and "uuid"+"settings"
OpenSearch fork tell: root JSON "version":{"distribution":"opensearch"} and .opensearch-* / .opendistro-* system indices in snapshot indices[]
fp_guards: http.title-style dorks over-match: port 9200 is also used by unrelated Java/HTTP apps and reverse proxies; require the JSON markers "tagline":"You Know, for Search" + "cluster_uuid", not just port:9200.

Auth-ENABLED clusters return HTTP 401 with body {"error":{"type":"security_exception","reason":"missing authentication credentials for REST request [/]"}} or [/_snapshot] -> NOT unauth; exclude with -"security_exception" -"missing authentication credentials". Surface open, access not exercised.

LBot / catch-all deception fleets echo Elasticsearch banners (fake cluster_name / version) to poison http.title and success-matchers; verify with a vendor-unique structural read: /_snapshot must return a JSON object keyed by repo names with real "type"+"uuid", and /_cluster/health must return {"status":"green|yellow|red","number_of_nodes":N}. A static/identical body across hosts = honeypot.

Amazon OpenSearch Service managed domains front the API behind IAM/SigV4 and return AccessDenied/403 on /_snapshot even when the banner looks open -> managed, not a self-hosted unauth target; exclude AWS ASNs / *.es.amazonaws.com.
Kibana/OpenSearch-Dashboards on 5601 is the UI, not the data API; do not treat a 5601 login page as the snapshot surface.
A snapshot present does NOT mean AI data present; do not label AI-scope on banner alone (gate via ai_scope_discriminator). Generic log/APM index backups are substrate, not in scope.

cves.desc: Directory traversal in Elasticsearch before 1.6.1 via the snapshot API repository path; unauthenticated remote arbitrary file read with JVM process privileges (Metasploit module exists).
Default dynamic scripting (MVEL) in Elasticsearch before 1.2 allows unauthenticated RCE via the _search API on the same open 9200 surface that exposes /_snapshot.
Groovy scripting sandbox bypass in Elasticsearch 1.3.0-1.3.7/1.4.0-1.4.2 yields unauthenticated RCE on the open REST API alongside the snapshot surface.
Unauthenticated StackOverflow DoS via crafted _search query string on the same open HTTP API (no auth required).
Unauthenticated OutOfMemory DoS: a moderate number of malformed HTTP requests forces an Elasticsearch node to exit.
Snapshot/repository private key stored on disk unencrypted; authenticated attacker can obtain S3/GCS repository credentials -> backup-store pivot.
Uncontrolled resource consumption evaluating crafted Mustache search templates crashes the node (DoS).

pivot_paths: GET / -> confirm banner: name, cluster_name, cluster_uuid, version.number, tagline 'You Know, for Search' (and version.distribution=opensearch for the fork).
GET /_cluster/health -> {status,number_of_nodes,active_shards} confirms a real live cluster (anti-honeypot).
GET /_snapshot (or /_cat/repositories?format=json) -> enumerate repository names + backend type (fs/s3/gcs/azure/url) + settings (bucket/location, NOT the data).
GET /_snapshot/{repo}/_all -> list snapshot names, uuids, state, start_time, and the indices[] array (names only) -> read which datasets were backed up.
GET /_cat/indices?format=json and GET /_aliases -> live index inventory to corroborate the snapshot indices[] (spot .ml-* / *knn* / *embeddings* names).
GET /_mapping (or /{ai-named-index}/_mapping) -> confirm dense_vector / sparse_vector / knn_vector field types = AI-scope gate satisfied.
GET /_ml/trained_models (ES) or GET /_plugins/_ml/models (OpenSearch) and GET /_ingest/pipeline -> deployed embedding/inference models = positive AI infrastructure attribution.
All read-only GETs only; stop at names + metadata.
restraint_note: Names-and-metadata only. Listing repositories, snapshot names, snapshot indices[], and field-type mappings is enumeration and is allowed. FORBIDDEN: GET /_snapshot/{repo}/{snapshot}/_restore-style or POST .../_restore (write primitive that mutates the cluster), POST /_snapshot (registers/overwrites a repository = write), and any attempt to download/stream the underlying repository object (fs path, S3/GCS/Azure bucket from settings) which is exfiltration of the backup contents. The index/snapshot names ARE the finding; the backed-up documents are not to be read or pulled. A `dense_vector` mapping confirms AI scope without ever fetching a vector. Surface open without exercised access stays labeled 'surface open, access not exercised.'
